content: Hi Sophie, I’ve been dragging all afternoon lately. I crash around 3 PM even when I sleep 7–8 hours, and I want to fix my energy.

Thanks for sharing that—afternoon crashes are really common, and we can definitely work on it. Let’s start by stabilizing your mornings with about 30g of protein at breakfast, like Greek yogurt plus eggs. That should help keep your blood sugar steadier into the afternoon.
I usually just grab toast and coffee at 8:30, then lunch is a sandwich or pasta. Could that be part of it?

Yes, that pattern can lead to a quick rise and drop in energy, especially with more refined carbs at lunch. Let’s swap toward protein, veggies, and slower carbs, and cut back on things like white pasta or white bread at midday. Keep your eating window between 8 AM and 6 PM for now so your routine stays consistent.
Got it. Also I’ve had low iron before, and I’m wondering if that’s happening again because I feel kind of weak during workouts.

That’s important to flag—low iron can absolutely affect energy and exercise tolerance. Let’s add iron-rich foods this week, especially spinach and lentils, and pair them with vitamin C foods when you can to help absorption. We’ll keep tracking symptoms while you make these nutrition changes.
Perfect, I can do that. Can we set a clear plan and check-in timeline so I stay on track?

Absolutely: daily breakfast with ~30g protein (Greek yogurt + eggs), reduce refined carbs at lunch, and follow the 8 AM–6 PM eating window. Add spinach or lentils most days for iron support, and log your afternoon energy and workout stamina. We’ll review progress weekly, and your blood panel is scheduled in 8 weeks to confirm how things are improving.
